title: Guess Whos Coming To Marry

jokes.text: Will's mom making him blow on his math homework like he's a little kid
jokes.setup: Will is doing homework when his mom comes to visit - seemingly normal interaction
jokes.punchline: She treats him like a child by making him literally blow on his math paper
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing the mother-son dynamic - she still sees him as her little boy despite his age. The awkwardness is earned through character relationship.

jokes.text: And if you value your tip, stop looking at my behind.
jokes.setup: Hilary giving instructions to catering staff
jokes.punchline: Her awareness and directness about being checked out
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary - vain but pragmatic. Good economy and character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Philip: 'I can't believe my baby sister's getting married.' Uncle Phil: 'I can. I just paid the florist.'
jokes.setup: Emotional sentiment about the wedding
jokes.punchline: Phil's practical/financial perspective cuts through the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Uncle Phil - always thinking about money. Clean setup/punchline with good character integration.

jokes.text: Vivian: 'Philip, all you ever think about is money.' Phil: 'No. That's not true, Vivian. Sometimes I think about other things.' [pause]
jokes.setup: Vivian accuses Phil of only thinking about money, Phil defends himself
jokes.punchline: His weak defense and the implication that he can't think of what those 'other things' are
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's priorities, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Viola: 'Would you two knock it off? You're married. It's time you start acting like it.' Phil: 'Viola, this is why I got married.'
jokes.setup: Viola tells the married couple to act married
jokes.punchline: Phil sarcastically suggests he married Vivian so Viola would tell them to act married
jokes.explanation: Nice sarcastic comeback from Phil, showing family dynamics and his wit.

jokes.text: Hilary: 'Like, I read about it, okay?'
jokes.setup: Discussion about women not needing to be married
jokes.punchline: Hilary's admission that her knowledge is purely theoretical from reading
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary - out of touch but trying to seem worldly. Great economy in the line.

jokes.text: Hillary: 'Lady, don't you know any other words?' Worker: 'No.'
jokes.setup: Hilary frustrated with worker saying 'no' repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Worker's literal response with another 'no'
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan response. The worker's literal interpretation creates perfect comedy through simplicity.

jokes.text: This is not a Ninja-Turtle wedding.
jokes.setup: Hilary rejecting pea-green patio chairs
jokes.punchline: Her comparison to Ninja Turtles shows her shallow but specific aesthetic standards
jokes.explanation: Great specific reference that captures both the color problem and Hilary's dramatic personality. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Miss Hillary, the day you do everything for yourself... will be the day I square-dance naked on the White-House lawn.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking if she has to do everything herself
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's impossible scenario expressing how unlikely that would be
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey line - specific, absurd, and perfectly captures his relationship with Hilary. The image is both ridiculous and memorable.

jokes.text: Mom, last time he came here... he pulled off Barbie's arm and burnt down her Dream House.
jokes.setup: Ashley defending why cousin Bobby is bad
jokes.punchline: Specific escalating examples of Bobby's destructiveness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from pulling off an arm to burning down the Dream House. Kid-specific humor that works.

jokes.text: Is this the Banks' house or Buckingham Palace?
jokes.setup: Helen arriving at the Banks house
jokes.punchline: Comparing their wealth to royalty
jokes.explanation: Standard wealth joke but establishes the class difference theme.

jokes.text: You got a boyfriend yet? Now ain't no point in saving nothing these days. You hear what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Helen greeting her sister
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual advice delivered loudly
jokes.explanation: Helen's loud, inappropriate character established. Cringe humor from oversharing.

jokes.text: The whole neighborhood hears what you saying.
jokes.setup: Helen's loud inappropriate comment
jokes.punchline: Will's observation about her volume
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Helen's loudness, Will serving as audience proxy.

jokes.text: Helen, I see you still haven't come out of your shell.
jokes.setup: Helen being extremely loud and outgoing
jokes.punchline: Vivian's sarcastic comment about Helen being shy
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic comment - Helen is obviously very outgoing. Good sisterly banter.

jokes.text: Bobby: 'I want to sleep with Carlton.' Will: 'I always dreamt when I heard those words it would be Paula Abdul saying them.'
jokes.setup: Innocent child wanting to sleep in Carlton's room
jokes.punchline: Will's sexual misinterpretation with specific celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith delivery - takes innocent statement and makes it sexual with specific pop culture reference. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Lester: 'Man, I take all the action I can get.'
jokes.setup: Phil asking about NFL action (betting)
jokes.punchline: Lester's response that could be about betting or sex
jokes.explanation: Double entendre establishing Lester's character as opportunistic.

jokes.text: Oh, yes. Speaking of taking care of business, does he?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Frank taking care of business
jokes.punchline: Viola asking about sexual performance in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate but character-consistent. Viola being blunt about sex.

jokes.text: Will is 17 years old. He's probably taking care of a little business himself. Aren't you, Will? [Will's shocked reaction] Absolutely not!
jokes.setup: Viola suggesting Will is sexually active
jokes.punchline: Will's emphatic denial despite being a teenage boy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Will caught off guard. His over-the-top denial adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Y'all don't know? Hey, I did teach her how to burp on command.
jokes.setup: Will being chosen as best man
jokes.punchline: His qualification is teaching his aunt to burp
jokes.explanation: Good childish reasoning for being best man. Shows Will's immaturity despite his age.

jokes.text: Hold on, Aunt Janice. Hold up. That was, like, 10 years ago. I'm, like, light-years ahead of you now. [Dancing badly]
jokes.setup: Will bragging about being a better dancer now
jokes.punchline: His dancing presumably being terrible despite his confidence
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment showing Will's overconfidence. Visual gag about his dancing skills.

jokes.text: Later on this afternoon, you ladies can go skating on it.
jokes.setup: Discussion of large engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Hilary suggesting it's so big you could ice skate on it
jokes.explanation: Good exaggeration about ring size. Fits Hilary's materialistic character.

jokes.text: The good, the bad, and the-- Don't even try it.
jokes.setup: Helen setting up 'good, bad, and ugly' reference
jokes.punchline: Viola cutting her off before she can call someone ugly
jokes.explanation: Nice interruption joke. Viola knows where this is going and stops it. Good economy.

jokes.text: Yeah, I would crack on your feet, but they don't reach all the way up here.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on feet needing pedicure
jokes.punchline: Will's height-based insult
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback about height difference. Shows Will's quick wit and trash-talking ability.

jokes.text: Well, I'm definitely getting married, and I want my wife to be a virgin. Well, you better hurry up, man. Mother Teresa ain't getting no younger.
jokes.setup: Carlton wanting to marry a virgin
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting Mother Teresa is his only option
jokes.explanation: Excellent roast of Carlton's unrealistic expectations. Dark but clever reference to Mother Teresa. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: She'll be an excellent homemaker and have the breasts the size of Australia.
jokes.setup: Carlton describing his ideal wife
jokes.punchline: His specific and ridiculous physical requirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - nerdy but horny. The Australia comparison is both specific and absurd.

jokes.text: I don't know, man. I just can't imagine... waking up to the same woman every day for the rest of my life... no matter how big Australia is.
jokes.setup: Will expressing doubts about marriage
jokes.punchline: Callback to Carlton's Australia breast reference
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the original joke while making Will's point about marriage.

jokes.text: Heck, by then young Carlton will be interviewing for preschool... and Carltonette will be a little croissant in the oven.
jokes.setup: Will imagining Carlton's future family
jokes.punchline: The name 'Carltonette' and pregnancy euphemism
jokes.explanation: Creative naming joke and cute pregnancy reference. Shows Will's imagination about Carlton's future.

jokes.text: Is that your feet, or did you break wind?
jokes.setup: Bad smell in the room
jokes.punchline: Will asking if it's Carlton's feet or gas
jokes.explanation: Basic bathroom humor. Gets a cheap laugh but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I'll be all dressed up with absolutely nothing to do. Just think of it as one of your dates.
jokes.setup: Carlton worried about having nothing to do as usher
jokes.punchline: Will comparing it to Carlton's unsuccessful dating life
jokes.explanation: Sharp roast of Carlton's dating life. Good economy and character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Forget it. I will not be an accomplice to the murder of Mickey Mouse.
jokes.setup: Bobby asking Carlton to take him to Disneyland
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dramatic refusal based on Bobby's destructive nature
jokes.explanation: Great exaggeration showing Carlton's fear of Bobby's destructiveness. Specific and memorable line.

jokes.text: You know, Bobby, I love it when you come to visit, man. They appreciate me so much more when you leave.
jokes.setup: Will talking to destructive Bobby
jokes.punchline: Admitting he only likes Bobby because it makes Will look better by comparison
jokes.explanation: Clever backhanded compliment showing Will's self-awareness and strategic thinking.

jokes.text: Cute, cute, cute, and cute. A dangerous combination.
jokes.setup: Frank meeting the Smith sisters
jokes.punchline: His assessment of them as dangerously attractive
jokes.explanation: Smooth line from Frank trying to charm all the sisters at once.

jokes.text: Man, what does she do when y'all eat out?
jokes.setup: Janice giving an overly generous tip
jokes.punchline: Will wondering about her tipping at restaurants
jokes.explanation: Logical follow-up question about Janice's generous tipping habit.

jokes.text: Here's $5. Go find your own.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about the promised lady
jokes.punchline: Will's dismissive response with small amount of money
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response. Will being dismissive with comically small amount.

jokes.text: My cousin used to date a girl who was...tall. Heck, the boys go to a predominantly...tall school.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to avoid saying 'white' about Frank
jokes.punchline: His awkward substitution of 'tall' for 'white'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant awkward moment showing Phil's discomfort with race. The euphemism is both obvious and painful.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, did I say white? I meant tall.
jokes.setup: Viola finally saying what everyone was thinking
jokes.punchline: Her fake correction back to the euphemism
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the euphemism setup. Viola breaking the tension by acknowledging the obvious.

jokes.text: She breast-fed me.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why he has to obey his mother
jokes.punchline: His ultimate reason being breastfeeding
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Will's ultimate loyalty to his mother. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: She breast-fed me.
jokes.setup: Will's mother drags him away from the wedding, he protests
jokes.punchline: His final desperate explanation for why he must obey
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and unexpectedness. Perfect explanation for why he can't disobey his mother. Highly memorable.

jokes.text: I don't care if Frank is black, white, or candy-apple red. I've been on Slim-Fast for the last two weeks to fit into my dress. Now somebody's gonna marry somebody up in here.
jokes.setup: Helen expressing frustration with the wedding drama
jokes.punchline: Her priorities being her diet and dress rather than racial issues
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Helen's priorities and practical nature. The 'candy-apple red' addition is perfect.

jokes.text: Do you remember the last reunion when Uncle Rubin came out of the closet? How could I forget? He came out wearing my purple-leather suit.
jokes.setup: Recalling Uncle Rubin coming out
jokes.punchline: The detail about him wearing Vivian's suit
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on coming out story - the personal affront of wearing her clothes adds humor.

jokes.text: She's the one that told Uncle Rubin he looked good in purple.
jokes.setup: Helen blaming Janice for family problems
jokes.punchline: Connecting it to encouraging Uncle Rubin's fashion choices
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending the Uncle Rubin joke while showing family dynamics.

jokes.text: Honey, who'd you think you were bringing him home to? Stevie Wonder?
jokes.setup: Janice saying she hoped no one would notice Frank was white
jokes.punchline: Helen's sarcastic reference to Stevie Wonder's blindness
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcastic response using specific celebrity reference. Sharp and quotable.

jokes.text: Then I guess I better pick up some Barry Manilow for the reception.
jokes.setup: Accepting that Frank is white and the wedding will happen
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical assumption about white people's music preferences
jokes.explanation: Plays on racial stereotypes about music. Gets a laugh but relies on cliché.

jokes.text: What's upstairs? The electric chair?
jokes.setup: The sisters' reluctance to go upstairs for snacks
jokes.punchline: Viola's dark comparison to execution
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about family tension. Viola's dramatic interpretation of reluctance.

jokes.text: More like quiet, evil, and colored.
jokes.setup: Reference to being 'loud, live, and in color'
jokes.punchline: Viola's cynical rewrite emphasizing racial tension
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that turns positive phrase negative. Shows Viola's bitter mood effectively.

jokes.text: Well, I guess she'll be on the first broom back to Philly.
jokes.setup: Viola leaving angry
jokes.punchline: Helen's witch reference with 'broom' instead of 'plane'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay calling Viola a witch. Good insult with creative transportation reference.

jokes.text: For a second there, I thought you was Wesley Snipes.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to get a tan
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated comparison to very dark-skinned actor
jokes.explanation: Good use of specific celebrity reference to comment on Frank's tanning attempt.

jokes.text: For a second there, I thought you was Wesley Snipes.
jokes.setup: Frank is trying to get a tan
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic comment about how dark he's gotten
jokes.explanation: Good topical reference to very dark-skinned actor. Will's sarcastic take on tanning efforts.

jokes.text: And when you come visit, you can borrow my Porsche.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to win over Will
jokes.punchline: His attempt to bribe with expensive car
jokes.explanation: Standard rich guy trying to buy affection joke. Predictable but fits character.

jokes.text: So you need any tips on sex? No, not really. Got any for me?
jokes.setup: Will offering sex advice to older man
jokes.punchline: Frank turning it around and asking Will for tips
jokes.explanation: Good reversal showing Frank's confidence and Will's inexperience despite his bravado.

jokes.text: Well, I just thought that I would, you know, drop in on the... on the thing.
jokes.setup: Will trying to avoid saying 'wedding'
jokes.punchline: His awkward euphemism avoiding the word his mother disapproves of
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Will's fear of his mother while trying to assert independence.

jokes.text: If I can just make it to that door.
jokes.setup: Will defying his mother and heading to the wedding
jokes.punchline: His prayer-like hope for survival in escaping
jokes.explanation: Good tension moment with Will fearing maternal retribution. Physical comedy setup.

jokes.text: Since Aunt Janice is of the Negroid persuasion... and Frank is of the Caucasoid persuasion... I've compiled a special medley of songs.
jokes.setup: Carlton announcing his musical contribution
jokes.punchline: His overly clinical/scientific terminology for race mixed with presumed bad song choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - overly academic approach to race mixed with terrible musical judgment. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I think you were deprived of oxygen at birth.
jokes.setup: Carlton playing his terrible medley
jokes.punchline: Will's medical explanation for Carlton's bad judgment
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with medical terminology. Shows Will's reaction to Carlton's terrible musical choices.

jokes.text: Why are you all looking at me? Reverend, go on with the ceremony. We are paying you by the hour.
jokes.setup: Minister asking if anyone objects, everyone looking at Phil
jokes.punchline: Phil's practical concern about hourly charges
jokes.explanation: Perfect Uncle Phil moment - everyone expects him to object but he's just worried about money.

tmdb_overview: Tensions spill over just before the wedding of Will's Aunt Janice when Will's mom meets Janice's intended - and forbids Will to attend the interracial wedding.
